description:
Advances: mutagen.mutation_pipeline

Discovered during S7 (mutagen-wrd.9) end-to-end test driver authoring.

## Problem

`lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex:411` — the production mix task constructs the MutationRunner input with a literal `test_modules: []`. `MutationLoop`'s per-site `Enum.each(test_modules, &ExUnit.Server.add_module/2)` is therefore a no-op against the empty list, so `ExUnit.run/0` inside the mutation pass reports zero tests run, and EVERY mutation is classified `:survived`.

## Reproduction

Run `mix mutagen --scope lib/foo.ex --tests test/foo_test.exs` against any module that has live mutators: `mutation.results[*].status` is uniformly `"survived"` regardless of whether the cited tests would actually kill those mutations.

## E2E driver workaround

The S7 driver's `mutation_collaborator/1` does an AST walk of each cited `_test.exs`, finds every `defmodule` block, and populates `test_modules` with `[{mod, %{async?: false, group: nil, parameterize: nil}}]` entries before delegating to `MutagenEx.MutationRunner.run/1`. The production caller still bypasses this.

## Spec reference

`mutagen.mutation_pipeline` r3 (mutation classification semantics) and r5 (`:killed` vs `:survived` semantics) — these rules cannot be exercised by the production wire-up while every result is forced to `:survived`.

## Resolution

In `lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex` around line 411, derive `test_modules` from the resolved `TestFilter.files` (or equivalent) rather than hardcoding `[]`. Retire the `mutation_collaborator` fork in `end_to_end_test.exs` once the production code is fixed.

## Related

- mutagen-wrd.9 (S7 end-to-end suite, where this was discovered)
- mutagen-wrd.11 (TestSelector exclude: [:test] fix — same wire-up surface)

IMPLEMENTED
Session: eb14f76b-662e-400e-ba6b-22e2f3772aea
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/eb14f76b-662e-400e-ba6b-22e2f3772aea.jsonl

Commit: 6cc625a
Stage branch: mutagen-wrd.12/mix-mutagen-test-modules
Stage worktree: /Users/mz/dev/mz/mutagen_ex-mix-mutagen-test-modules
Based on: 258665a (epic/mutagen-wrd at mutagen-wrd.11 head, per orchestrator briefing)

Files touched:
- lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex — phase_mutation/7 now derives test_modules
  from test_filter.files via MutagenEx.TestModuleDiscovery.discover/1
  instead of hardcoding [].
- lib/mutagen_ex/test_module_discovery.ex (NEW) — pure-AST walk of cited
  *_test.exs files; returns [{module, %{async?: false, group: nil,
  parameterize: nil}}]. Unreadable/unparseable files contribute zero
  entries without raising.
- test/mutagen_ex/end_to_end_test.exs — retired the mutation_collaborator
  fork + its discover_test_modules/1 + alias_to_module/1 helpers per
  the ticket's Resolution clause; dispatch now points at
  MutagenEx.MutationRunner.run/1 directly. Moduledoc updated to match.
- test/mutagen_ex/test_module_discovery_test.exs (NEW) — 12 unit tests.
- test/mutagen_ex/json_reporter_test.exs — added a regression test that
  Mix.Tasks.Mutagen.run/2 hands MutationRunner a non-empty test_modules
  derived from test_filter.files. Would have failed pre-fix.

Tests added:
- MutagenEx.TestModuleDiscoveryTest (12 tests): empty input, single +
  multiple defmodules, multi-file ordering, dotted aliases, non-_test.exs
  files, nested defmodule blocks, missing/unparseable/no-defmodule files
  (each returns [] without raising), and the locked-in
  `%{async?: false, group: nil, parameterize: nil}` cfg shape.
- MutagenEx.JsonReporterTest "mix task hands MutationRunner a populated
  test_modules derived from test_filter.files (mutagen-wrd.12)" — uses
  the dispatch capture pattern to assert phase_mutation/7's input to
  the mutation collaborator.

Verification stubs flipped: none — mutagen.mutation_pipeline's existing
v2 stub (`mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_test.exs`) is the
recorded gate for r5; both v2 and the e2e suite remain `execute: true`
and continue to pass. No new stub block was added because the spec text
already covers the killed/survived contract this ticket restores; the
fix is a production-wiring repair, not a new behavioral invariant.

Smoke (mix compile --warnings-as-errors + mix test):
- default suite: 321 tests, 0 failures (was 308 in baseline; +13 from
  the new test_module_discovery_test + json_reporter regression test)
- mix test --only e2e_slow: 13 tests, 0 failures, 2 honest skips (the
  same two skips that existed pre-change: mutagen-wrd.13's ecto_user
  scenario and mutagen-wrd.15/.16's literal-mutator-on-decisions.ex
  scenario 2a). All eleven non-skipped e2e scenarios now go through
  the production MutationRunner.run/1, proving the production wire-up
  reproduces what the prior mutation_collaborator fork demonstrated.

Summary: `Mix.Tasks.Mutagen.phase_mutation/7` no longer hands
MutationRunner an empty `test_modules` list. The mix task now uses
`MutagenEx.TestModuleDiscovery.discover/1` (extracted from the e2e
driver's prior workaround) to walk each cited test file's AST for
`defmodule` blocks and package them in the `{module, %{async?: false,
group: nil, parameterize: nil}}` shape `ExUnit.Server.add_module/2`
consumes. With MutationLoop's per-site re-registration loop now
operating on a real list, the `:killed` vs `:survived` distinction
in `mutagen.mutation_pipeline.r5` is observable through the production
pipeline for the first time. The e2e suite's `mutation_collaborator`
fork is gone — the production runner is what every scenario exercises.

VERIFY: PASS
Session: a57ed5c6ef54edc8c (subagent of eb14f76b-662e-400e-ba6b-22e2f3772aea)
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/eb14f76b-662e-400e-ba6b-22e2f3772aea/subagents/agent-a57ed5c6ef54edc8c.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 5
  - lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex
  - lib/mutagen_ex/test_module_discovery.ex (NEW)
  - test/mutagen_ex/end_to_end_test.exs
  - test/mutagen_ex/json_reporter_test.exs
  - test/mutagen_ex/test_module_discovery_test.exs (NEW)
- Files outside Allowed globs: 0. Ticket lists no explicit "Allowed touches" / "Out of Scope (files)" globs; the Resolution clause is the authoritative gate. It names lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex and end_to_end_test.exs directly; the new TestModuleDiscovery module + its tests are a clean extraction of the Resolution's "derive test_modules from TestFilter.files" requirement; the json_reporter regression test exercises the exact wire-up the Resolution mandates. The surface declared by mutation_pipeline.spec.md does not list lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex, but that file is the cli spec's surface and the ticket's Resolution names it line-explicitly. Cross-spec touch is intentional and authorized.
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0. Mutation_pipeline spec's "Out of scope" enumerates (a) mutator catalog and (b) JSON document production; the diff touches neither.

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 4, passed: 4
  - v1 (baseline_test.exs): 8 tests, 0 failures
  - v2 (mutation_runner_test.exs): 18 tests, 0 failures
  - v3 (integration/c1_test.exs) + v4 (integration/c2_test.exs): 2 tests, 0 failures
- Requirements coverage (Advances: mutagen.mutation_pipeline; r5 is the only must requirement the ticket's code path materially affects):
  - r5 (5-outcome classification, killed vs survived distinction observable through production): covered by test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_test.exs:373 (":killed when ExUnit reports failures > 0") and :386 (":survived when failures == 0") for the runner-level contract, AND by test/mutagen_ex/json_reporter_test.exs:715 ("mix task hands MutationRunner a populated test_modules…") for the production-wiring contract this ticket actually changed. Would fail if phase_mutation/7 reverted to test_modules: [] — the regression test pins the post-discover module atom into the captured input and refute-asserts non-empty. Would also fail if TestModuleDiscovery returned the wrong cfg shape — the discovery_test.exs:234 cfg-shape contract pins %{async?: false, group: nil, parameterize: nil} exactly.
  - r1, r2, r3, r4, r6, r7, r8, r9, r10, r11: not materially altered by this ticket. r3/r5 are the requirements the ticket text cites; r3 in the spec is actually "self-mutation refused" (the ticket text says "r3 (mutation classification semantics)" but r3 covers self-mutation refusal; this is a minor ticket-text mislabel, the actual fix targets r5). r3 remains covered by mutation_runner_test.exs self-mutation refusal tests; the diff does not touch that path.
- Intent-level scope concerns: none. The fix is a pure wire-up repair: phase_mutation/7 now calls TestModuleDiscovery.discover(test_filter.files) where it formerly hardcoded []. No mutator catalog change, no JSON schema change, no MutationRunner / MutationLoop / Baseline behavior change.
- Project verification: pass. make smoke = 1 doctest + 321 tests + 0 failures (13 e2e_slow excluded). Full e2e_slow suite separately = 13 tests, 0 failures, 2 pre-existing honest skips. Implementer's claim of 321 default + 13 e2e_slow with the existing 2 skips matches.
- Audit-expanded scope (per step 11): none — no prior AUDIT comments on this ticket.

Verdict: PASS

Notes (advisory):
- The new TestModuleDiscovery module lives at lib/mutagen_ex/test_module_discovery.ex but isn't listed in mutation_pipeline.spec.md's surface. The surface block should arguably be extended (or the new module should land in mutation_pipeline's surface list) so future scope checks find it under spec.validate. Not a verify-blocker — the spec text already covers the killed/survived contract and the new module is a pure derivation helper.
- Ticket description has a minor textual error: it cites "r3 (mutation classification semantics) and r5". r3 in the spec is self-mutation refusal; r5 is the 5-outcome classification. The implementer's fix correctly targets r5; the ticket text mislabels r3.
- The implementer's comment claims "mix test --only e2e_slow: 13 tests, 0 failures, 2 honest skips" — confirmed by direct run (re-ran here, matched).
- Nested-defmodule discovery picks up inner helper modules (test_module_discovery_test.exs:155 acknowledges this). The implementer notes ExUnit.Server.add_module on a non-test module is harmless; the unit test pins the behavior so any future filter-by-use-ExUnit.Case decision is a deliberate change. Acceptable.
- The discover/1 fn does NOT filter by *_test.exs suffix (test_module_discovery_test.exs:138 documents this as intentional — the caller's filtering is the gate). Lines up with how phase_mutation/7 receives a pre-filtered test_filter.files from TestSelector.

AUDIT: APPROVE
Session: ad73979a330913e5e (subagent of eb14f76b-662e-400e-ba6b-22e2f3772aea)
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/eb14f76b-662e-400e-ba6b-22e2f3772aea/subagents/agent-ad73979a330913e5e.jsonl

Reasoning: The collapse of r1-r4 and r6-r11 into "not materially altered" is
honest, not rubber-stamping. The lib-side diff is exactly two files
(lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex +9/-2 and the new
lib/mutagen_ex/test_module_discovery.ex), and the mutation_pipeline surface
(baseline.ex, mutation_runner.ex, mutation_runner/mutation_loop.ex) is
byte-untouched — confirmed via `git diff 258665a HEAD -- lib/mutagen_ex/`
which yields no changes to any of the three surface files. So r1 (Baseline
abort), r2 (max_cases/seed/async warning), r3 (self_mutation_refused), r4
(timeout + tainted_predecessors), r6 (restore via Code.compile_quoted), r7
(snapshot growth), r8 (state_drift_warning), r9 (stderr capture), r10
(no test reload), and r11 (no FS writes) all have their implementation code
unchanged; v1+v2+v3+v4 stubs all re-ran green (8+18+2 = 28 tests).

r5 is the only requirement materially affected, and it is doubly covered:
the existing runner-level contract at test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_test.exs:373
(:killed) and :386 (:survived), and the new production-wiring regression
at test/mutagen_ex/json_reporter_test.exs:715 which captures
phase_mutation/7's input via dispatch injection and asserts both
`refute test_modules == []` and the exact `{Mutagen.Phase.MutationCaptureTest,
%{async?: false, group: nil, parameterize: nil}}` tuple is present. The
regression test is genuinely load-bearing — reverting phase_mutation/7 to
test_modules: [] would flip the refute. The cfg-shape contract is pinned
at test/mutagen_ex/test_module_discovery_test.exs:234.

The r3 ticket-text mislabel is a pure citation error: the ticket says
"r3 (mutation classification semantics) and r5" but the spec's r3 is
self-mutation refusal and the spec's r5 is the 5-outcome classification.
The fix correctly targets r5 (it does not touch the MutagenEx.* /
Mix.Tasks.Mutagen refusal path in MutationRunner.run/1). r3's actual
contract is untouched in the diff.

Mechanical re-counts: 5 files touched (matches verifier's count). Files
outside Allowed: 0 — the ticket's Resolution clause explicitly names
lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex and end_to_end_test.exs. The new
TestModuleDiscovery module is a clean extraction of the Resolution's
"derive test_modules from TestFilter.files" requirement; the json_reporter
regression test exercises the exact wire-up the Resolution mandates. The
mutation_pipeline spec's "Out of scope" enumerates (a) mutator catalog and
(b) JSON document production; the diff touches neither. The new module
is not listed in any spec's surface block — this is the "Note (advisory)"
the verifier flagged, correctly downgraded because the spec text already
covers the killed/survived contract and this is a pure derivation helper.

Project verification independently re-run: mix test = 1 doctest + 321
tests + 0 failures (13 e2e_slow excluded); mix test --only e2e_slow =
13 tests + 0 failures + 2 pre-existing honest skips. Both match the
implementer's and verifier's claims.

No scope violations against ticket intent. No requirement was tested by
a test that passes for orthogonal reasons. No happy-path-only coverage of
an error-state requirement. The verifier's collapse correctly reflects
the diff's narrow scope.

title: DISCOVERY: mix mutagen passes hardcoded test_modules: [] to MutationRunner
